glost: noun. Lead glazing used for pottery.
gor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Gorontalo.
gory: adjective. Covered with blood; very bloody.
got: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Gothic.
goy: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Goundo.
gyron: noun. A triangular form having an angle at the fess point and the opposite side at the edge of the escutcheon.
gyros: noun. Alternative form of gyro (“Greek sandwich”).
logy: adjective. Slow to respond or react; lethargic.
long: adjective. Having much distance in space from one end to the other.
lory: noun. Any of various small, brightly coloured parrots native to Australasia.
lost: verb. simple past and past participle of lose
lot: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Lotuko.
nor: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Norwegian.
nos: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eastern Nisu.
nosy: adjective. Prying, inquisitive or curious in other’s affairs; tending to snoop or meddle.
not: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nomatsiguenga.
ons: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ono.
orgy: noun. A secret rite or ceremony, typically involving riotous and dissolute behavior, including dancing, drunkenness and indiscriminate
ors: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Orang Seletar.
ort: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Adivasi Odia.
rot: verb. To suffer decomposition due to biological action, especially by fungi or bacteria.
rotl: noun. Alternative form of rottol: a former Middle Eastern and North African unit of weight, usually 1–5 pounds (0.5–2.5 kg).
rots: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of rot
ryot: noun. A farmer or tiller of the soil.
slog: noun. A long, tedious walk or march.
slot: noun. A narrow depression, perforation, or aperture; especially, one for the reception of a piece fitting or sliding into it.
snort: noun. The sound made by exhaling or inhaling roughly through the nose.
snot: noun. Mucus, especially mucus from the nose.
sol: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Solos.
son: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Songhay languages.
song: noun. A musical composition with lyrics for voice or voices, performed by singing.
sonly: adjective. Of, pertaining to, or characteristic of a son.
sorn: verb. to impose upon another for food and lodging.
sot: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Sotho.
story: noun. An account of real or fictional events.
strong: adjective. Capable of producing great physical force.
strongly: adverb. In a strong or powerful manner.
sty: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Siberian Tatar.
syn: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Senaya.
tog: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tonga (Malawi).
ton: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tongan.
tong: noun. An instrument or tool used for manipulating things in a fire without touching them with the hands.
tongs: noun. An instrument or tool used for picking things up without touching them with the hands or fingers, consisting of two slats or grips
tor: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Togbo-Vara Banda.
tory: noun. Alternative letter-case form of Tory.
troy: adjective. Of, or relating to, troy weight.
try: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Turung.
yon: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Yongkom.
